description: This schematic figure compares the slip reversibility difference between solution-treated (ST) and aged Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel samples under cyclic loading with critical resolved shear stress τc. Subfigure (a) illustrates the ST condition: short-range ordering (SRO) structures exist in the austenitic matrix, and repeated dislocation shearing leads to premature, localized SRO destruction, inducing non-uniform softening and local strain concentration, which results in low slip reversibility. Subfigure (b) illustrates the aged condition: uniformly dispersed coherent L12-structured intragranular κ-carbides are present in the matrix. Dislocation shearing of these precipitates creates anti-phase boundaries and induces glide plane softening, promoting homogeneous planar slip band formation and distributing plastic strain evenly across multiple slip systems. This effect mitigates localized strain concentration, significantly improves dislocation motion reversibility under cyclic loading, explaining the superior fatigue performance of aged samples compared to ST samples.
